original_hypothesis.title: Transient storage parameter passing may fail on chains/EVM configs without stable tstore/tload semantics, bricking market deployments
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: HooksFactory uses TransientBytesArray (tstore/tload) to pass TmpMarketParameterStorage to the WildcatMarket constructor via factory.getMarketParameters(). This assumes Cancun/EIP-1153 transient storage is supported and behaves consistently. On chains that lag Cancun support, are configured differently, or have non-standard execution environments, market deployment could fail or return incorrect parameters, leading to misinitialized immutables (asset/borrower/sentinel/etc.) and potentially irrecoverable deployment failures.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational chain-compatibility risk. Wildcat targets multiple chains; any mismatch in EVM version support can cause systemic deployment failures or misconfiguration, impacting production operations broadly.
deployment_assumptions: Deployment occurs on a chain or L2 that does not support transient storage (EIP-1153) or has divergent semantics
Factory and market are compiled with evm_version=cancun assumptions (as in foundry.toml/hardhat.config.ts)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: foundry.toml sets evm_version='cancun' and solc=0.8.25; TransientBytesArray.sol uses tload/tstore directly without feature detection.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Direct use of tload/tstore for dynamic bytes encoding/decoding; no runtime feature gating

preconditions: Target chain does not fully support Cancun transient storage opcodes or has bugs in their implementation
HooksFactory is used as the canonical deployment path for markets (as designed)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Critical deployment wiring relies on transient storage opcodes; failure breaks all new market deployments
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployment tooling chooses compiler/evmVersion; chain choice matters

operational_questions: Which target chains (Base/Arbitrum/Polygon) fully support EIP-1153 at the intended deployment times?
Are there chain-specific audits/tests for tstore/tload behavior under reverts and nested calls?
Is there a fallback non-transient deployment path?
validation_requirements: Deploy and create markets on each target chain/testnet with identical bytecode
Chaos testing: force reverts mid-deploy and ensure tmp parameters cannot leak across calls
Verify market constructor reads correct 0x260 bytes from factory staticcall under all conditions

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Select a chain/environment with partial Cancun support or custom EVM behavior
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attempt deployMarket; observe misreads or deployment reverts
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Operationally, this manifests as an inability to onboard borrowers/markets on that chain
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Not an attacker-driven exploit in normal conditions; environment-driven

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: High: market deployment pipeline bricked on affected networks
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High: requires redeploying factory with alternative parameter passing or changing chain strategy

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Deployment reverts in market constructor when calling factory.getMarketParameters() (returndatasize mismatch)
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Markets deployed with incorrect immutable params (asset/borrower/sentinel mismatch) — catastrophic if happens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
